text.title: On-Call Rotation Bootstrap Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the On-Call Rotation Bootstrap methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Publish a one-page On-Call Charter to the team wiki before the first shift. It states: shift length, shift boundaries, participating engineers, acknowledgement SLA, escalation tree (primary + secondary + manager), comp policy, swap policy, named rotation owner.

rationale.source: Google SRE Book Chapter 11; PagerDuty Operations Maturity Model

rule.rationale: An undocumented rotation defaults to whatever the loudest engineer remembers from their last job. The charter is the contract that prevents loudest-engineer drift.

rule.statement: Define an acknowledgement SLA per severity (sev-1=5min, sev-2=15min, sev-3=no SLA). Escalation to secondary fires automatically when primary misses the ack window. The SLA is enforced in the paging tool, not by social pressure.
rule.rationale: Tool-enforced escalation removes cognitive load on the primary ('am I OK to go to sleep?') and protects customer MTTR.

rule.statement: Default to a two-tier rotation: primary + secondary per shift. The secondary covers primary unavailability, takes over after 4 consecutive pages, and is the second pair of eyes on sev-1. Single-engineer rotation is allowed ONLY for teams of 5-7 with an external SRE retainer.

rationale.source: SRE workbook on cognitive load; common burnout postmortems

rule.rationale: Single-engineer rotation under unbounded page load is the single highest-correlation cause of on-call resignations.

rule.statement: Off-hours work during on-call accrues compensation: typically 1 hour of time-off-in-lieu per off-hours page-and-respond, capped per shift, with cash payout above the cap. Policy documented in the HR handbook with signatures from engineering leader and HR.

rationale.source: research on burnout in on-call rotations; EU/UK/Brazil labour-law on after-hours work

rule.rationale: Unpaid 3 AM pages are the single most-cited driver of on-call resignations. Comp must be automatic, defaulted-on, and explicit in the handbook.

rule.statement: Define red-line thresholds: >2 off-hours pages/shift OR >3 weekend pages/quarter triggers a load-reduction action (alert tuning, runbook automation, headcount, temporary external SRE). The red-line is checked weekly and reported monthly.

rationale.source: Google SRE Book on on-call sustainability; toil-budget concept

rule.rationale: Without a red-line, on-call load drifts upward as alerts proliferate. The red-line forces action when load crosses a quantitative threshold.

rule.statement: Every new rotation member completes 2 shadow shifts (silent observer on secondary) + 2 supervised solo shifts (primary with the previous primary as named safety-net) before a true solo shift. The paging tool blocks solo primary assignment without a completed shadow record.
rule.rationale: Throwing new joiners into solo primary on day one spikes MTTR; the shadow period internalizes runbook conventions and the paging tool muscle memory.
